title: User-Friendly 500 Error Page for Production
story: As a DataTools Pro user, I want to see a friendly, branded error page when a server error occurs in production, so that I understand something went wrong without seeing technical error details.
description:
In staging, the full Symfony error page should be visible for debugging. However, in production, 500 errors should be intercepted and the user redirected to a user-friendly branded error page instead of showing raw stack traces or technical details.

## Requirements

- Set Laravel environment to `production` with `APP_DEBUG=false`.
- Override the default 500 error template with a branded page.
- Capture invalid URLs and display the same friendly error page.
- Hide technical error details (status codes, stack traces) from end users.
acceptance_criteria: Production 500 errors display a branded, user-friendly error page
No stack traces or technical details are shown to end users
Staging environment continues to show detailed Symfony error pages
Invalid URLs are captured and redirect to the error page
APP_DEBUG is set to false in production .env
ux_changes: Custom branded 500 error page replaces the default Laravel/Symfony error page in production. Page hides all technical details.
middleware_changes: Laravel .env configured with APP_ENV=production and APP_DEBUG=false. Error handler overrides the default error template.
implementation_notes: Set APP_ENV=production and APP_DEBUG=false in .env. Laravel's default error handling was customized by overwriting the error templates. Production now shows a clean branded error page while staging retains the full Symfony debug page.
comment_summary: Developer set the environment to production with debug disabled and the default Laravel error page showed a clean template. Product owner approved the approach and confirmed the templates can be further customized.
